# License and Activation

RiverDrop is a premium application. While you can browse your local files without a license, an active license key is required to connect to remote servers and use advanced features.

## Entering Your License Key

After purchasing RiverDrop from [Gumroad](https://gumroad.com), you will receive an email containing your **License Key**.

1.  Launch RiverDrop.
2.  In the activation screen that appears, enter your **License Key**.
3.  Click **Activate**.

![Activation Screen](images/activation-screen.png){fig-alt="Entering the license key in the activation screen"}

## Activation and Offline Usage

RiverDrop validates your license key against the Gumroad API. Once validated, your license is stored securely in your Mac's **Keychain**.

### Offline Grace Period
We understand that scientists and researchers often work in environments with intermittent internet access (e.g., in the field or secure facilities). RiverDrop includes a **7-day offline grace period**. As long as your license was successfully validated online within the last 7 days, you can continue to use all features offline.

::: {.callout-warning}
If you remain offline for more than 7 days, RiverDrop will prompt you to reconnect to the internet to refresh your license status.
:::

## Deactivation

To move your license to a new Mac, you can deactivate your current installation.

1.  Open **Settings** (⌘+,).
2.  Navigate to the **License** tab.
3.  Click **Deactivate**.

This will remove your license from the local Keychain and allow you to re-activate RiverDrop on another machine.

::: {.callout-tip}
If you lose access to your machine and cannot deactivate, please contact support with your purchase email.
:::
